parameters.formDescription: Upload a tender / RFT PDF to extract structured data and make it chat-searchable.

categories.name: project_summary
categories.description: Cover page content including RFT number, contract number, project title, issuing authority name, contact person name, phone, email and document date.

categories.name: scope_and_timeline
categories.description: Purpose of procurement, scope of work, deliverables, specifications overview and all key dates including issue date, questions deadline, submission deadline, award date and delivery date.

categories.name: submission_rules
categories.description: Instructions for bidders, how to submit the tender, mandatory submission requirements, addenda rules, alteration and withdrawal rules and contact for questions.

categories.name: compliance_terms
categories.description: Terms and conditions including insurance requirements, WSIB, health and safety, AODA accessibility, conflict of interest, indemnification and contract termination clauses.

categories.name: specification_checklist
categories.description: Itemized specification form with list of required features or items. Each item has a description and Yes or No compliance indicator with technical dimensions and material standards.

categories.name: bid_form
categories.description: Form of tender, supplier declaration, pricing table, HST registration number, WSIB account number, signature block and irrevocability statement.

nodes.name: Extract structured data from a document

parameters.content:
## 🧬 Pipeline 2 — RAG Ingestion
Splits the document into semantic sections, chunks each section along paragraph boundaries (never mid-clause), embeds every chunk, and stores it in Supabase tagged with a doc_id.

**Nodes:** Split a file + Parse a document → Merge → Prepare RAG Payload → Carve Text by Section → Loop Over Items → Gemini Embed → Insert Embed Vectors

## 📊 Pipeline 1 — Document Intelligence & Tracker
Parses the uploaded PDF, extracts key structured fields, and logs one row per document to a Google Sheets tracker.

**Nodes:** Parse a document → Extract structured data → Normalize + Bid Score → Tender Tracker

options.system:
You are a tender document assistant for [YOUR COMPANY NAME]. Your job is to extract and present information from tender documents accurately.

Rules:
- Answer ONLY from the provided context
- ALWAYS structure your answer as bullet points
- Include ALL relevant details from the context
- Never summarize or omit details
- If information appears in multiple sources, 
  combine them into one complete answer
- Always cite which section the answer came from
- If information is not in context, say exactly:
  "This information is not in the selected tender document"
- Never add information not present in the context

parameters.content:
## 💬 Pipeline 3 — Conversational Chat (WhatsApp)
Independently triggered by an incoming message

**Nodes:** WhatsApp Trigger → Extract Question → Gemini Embed Question → Vector Search (Supabase RPC) → Build Context → DeepSeek/LLM Answer → Format Reply → Send a text message

## 🗂️ AI Tender Document Analyser (Powered by LlamaParse + RAG)
This workflow turns a long tender/RFT PDF into structured tracker data AND a searchable chat interface — in one pipeline. Upload a document once; extract it automatically and ask it questions forever after.

### 🧩 Why Semantic Chunking Is Important Here
Most RAG workflows split documents by raw character count, which cuts legal clauses in half mid-sentence (a dollar amount ends up in one chunk, its conditions in the next). This workflow instead:
• Uses LlamaParse's embedded page markers to extract EXACT section text (not an approximated line count)
• Splits along paragraph/clause boundaries so every chunk stays one complete, self-contained requirement
• This single change took retrieval accuracy from "context does not contain this information" to 100% on validated test questions

### 🔍 What It Does
This workflow:
• Accepts a tender PDF via form upload
• Classifies it, then extracts structured fields (deadlines, insurance, compliance, contacts) into a Google Sheet
• Splits the document into semantic sections and stores embedded chunks in Supabase, isolated per document
• Powers a Telegram chat bot that answers questions about the document, citing the exact section every time

### ⚙️ How It Works (High-level)
• User uploads a PDF via the form
• LlamaParse Platform parses, classifies, and extracts structured fields
• Extracted fields are logged to a Google Sheets tracker
• In parallel, the document is semantically chunked and embedded 
• Chunks are stored in Supabase (pgvector) tagged with a doc_id
• A Whatsapp message triggers the chat pipeline: question → embedded → vector search scoped to doc_id → context assembled → LLM answers strictly from that context → reply sent back to Whatsapp

### 🧠 Key Capabilities
• Structure-aware parsing (not basic OCR) via LlamaParse Platform
• Semantic, paragraph-aligned chunking — no clause ever split across chunks
• Document-isolated vector search — multiple tenders never bleed into one answer
• Deterministic answers — same question returns the same complete answer every run
• Explicit "not in document" response instead of hallucinating when information is missing
• Every answer cites the section it came from

### 📦 Requirements
• LlamaParse API key  (cloud.llamaindex.ai)
• Google Gemini API key (embeddings)
• Supabase project with pgvector enabled 
• Google Sheets OAuth2 credential
• Anthropic-compatible or DeepSeek chat completion credential (used for answer generation)

### 💡 Example Use Cases
• Government/municipal RFT and tender review for contractors
• Any procurement team that reviews multiple RFPs/RFQs weekly
• Turning any dense legal/compliance PDF into a queryable knowledge base
• Cross-referencing scope, insurance, and deadline requirements without re-reading the source document

### 🎯 Output
• Structured Google Sheets row per document (for tracking/comparison)
• Conversational Whatsapp interface, permanently searchable per document
• Source-cited, deterministic answers — never a guess

main.node: Extract structured data from a document
